departments.name: Computer Science
departments.description: UTEP's CS department trains AI researchers, software engineers, and cybersecurity experts. The department has strong partnerships with national labs and defense organizations in the region.
fun_facts: UTEP is one of the top universities in the nation for awarding CS degrees to Hispanic students.
UTEP CS students regularly intern at companies like Google, Microsoft, IBM, and NASA's Jet Propulsion Lab.
UTEP's cybersecurity program helps protect critical infrastructure along the U.S.-Mexico border.

connections.robot: Robots use AI and programming — exactly what UTEP CS students learn to build!
connections.computer: Every app, game, and website starts with code — and UTEP trains world-class coders.
connections.brain: AI tries to think like a brain — UTEP researchers build intelligent systems that learn and adapt.
connections.gear: Gears make machines work, and software engineering at UTEP makes digital systems work.

departments.name: Electrical & Computer Engineering
departments.description: UTEP's ECE department works on power grids, wireless tech, and chip design. Researchers develop smart energy solutions for the growing El Paso–Juárez region.
fun_facts: UTEP engineers are developing smart power grid technology to bring reliable electricity to remote border communities.
UTEP's ECE labs have equipment worth millions of dollars for testing circuits and wireless systems.
El Paso gets over 300 days of sunshine a year, and UTEP engineers are harnessing it with solar energy research.

connections.lightning: Lightning is pure electricity — UTEP electrical engineers study how to harness and control electrical power!
connections.battery: Batteries store energy, and UTEP engineers design better energy systems for the future.

departments.name: Civil Engineering
departments.description: UTEP civil engineers design roads, bridges, and water systems for the El Paso–Juárez binational region, one of the largest border metro areas in the world.
fun_facts: UTEP civil engineering students have won national bridge-building competitions, designing tiny structures that hold thousands of pounds.
El Paso and Juárez share water from the Rio Grande — UTEP engineers help manage this critical shared resource.
UTEP researchers design earthquake-resistant buildings suited for the desert Southwest geology.

connections.bridge: Bridges connect communities — UTEP engineers design real bridges that link El Paso and Juárez!
connections.building: Every building starts with engineering — UTEP students learn to design structures that are safe and strong.
connections.house: The house you live in was designed by engineers — UTEP trains the people who build our cities.

departments.name: Mechanical Engineering
departments.description: UTEP mechanical engineers design everything from aerospace parts to 3D-printed medical devices. The department partners with NASA, Fort Bliss, and White Sands Missile Range.
fun_facts: UTEP students have designed and launched actual rockets through NASA's University Student Launch Initiative.
UTEP's Formula SAE team designs and races a full car from scratch every year, competing against top engineering schools.
UTEP has one of the most advanced 3D-printing labs in the country, printing metal parts for aerospace and medical use.

connections.car: Cars are feats of mechanical engineering — UTEP's SAE team builds a real race car every year!
connections.airplane: Airplanes fly thanks to mechanical engineering — UTEP partners with NASA on aerospace research!
connections.rocket: Rockets need precision engineering — UTEP students have launched real rockets with NASA!

departments.name: Metallurgical, Materials & Biomedical Engineering
departments.description: This unique UTEP department studies the materials that make up everything around us — from metals and ceramics to materials used inside the human body like implants and prosthetics.
fun_facts: UTEP researchers 3D-print titanium parts that can be used inside the human body as bone replacements.
UTEP's materials science program is one of the oldest and most respected in the southwestern U.S.
UTEP scientists study how desert heat and salt corrode bridges and pipelines, then design materials that last longer.

connections.diamond: Diamonds are one of the strongest materials on Earth — UTEP materials engineers study what makes materials strong!
connections.bone: UTEP engineers 3D-print materials that can replace broken bones inside the human body!

departments.name: Biological Sciences
departments.description: UTEP biologists study the Chihuahuan Desert, one of the most biodiverse deserts on Earth. Researchers track endangered species, discover new organisms, and study how life adapts to extreme heat.
fun_facts: The Chihuahuan Desert — right outside UTEP — has more plant species than any other desert in North America.
UTEP biologists have discovered species of desert insects and plants that were previously unknown to science.
UTEP's Biodiversity Collections house over 800,000 preserved specimens from the border region.

connections.cactus: Cacti thrive in the desert — UTEP biologists study how desert plants survive extreme heat and drought!
connections.flower: Flowers are part of complex ecosystems — UTEP researchers study how desert plants and pollinators depend on each other!
connections.fish: The Rio Grande supports endangered fish found nowhere else — UTEP biologists lead efforts to protect them!
connections.butterfly: Monarch butterflies migrate through El Paso — UTEP researchers track their incredible 2,000-mile journey!
connections.cat: All cats share DNA with wild desert predators — UTEP biologists study how animals evolve and adapt!
connections.dog: Dogs evolved from wolves over thousands of years — UTEP biologists study animal evolution and genetics!

departments.name: Chemistry & Biochemistry
departments.description: UTEP chemists develop new medicines, study environmental pollutants, and do forensic analysis. The department has strong research in drug discovery for diseases that affect border communities.
fun_facts: UTEP chemistry researchers are developing new medicines to fight diseases like diabetes, which affects many border residents.
UTEP's forensic science program trains students to analyze crime scene evidence using chemistry techniques.
UTEP chemists study air quality in El Paso, monitoring dust and pollution that blows across the border.

connections.fire: Fire is a chemical reaction — UTEP chemists study how molecules react, break apart, and form new substances!
connections.water: Clean water is a chemistry challenge — UTEP researchers develop ways to purify water for border communities!

departments.name: Physics
departments.description: UTEP physicists study the universe — from subatomic particles to distant galaxies. El Paso's clear desert skies make it an ideal location for astronomical observation.
fun_facts: UTEP physicists collaborate with CERN, the world's largest particle physics lab in Switzerland.
El Paso's clear skies and low light pollution make it one of the best places in Texas for stargazing and space research.
UTEP physics students have contributed to research on dark matter and the origins of the universe.

connections.star: Stars are giant nuclear furnaces — UTEP physicists study how they form, shine, and eventually explode!
connections.moon: The moon shapes our tides and nights — UTEP astrophysicists study how celestial bodies interact!
connections.sun: The sun powers all life on Earth — UTEP physicists study solar energy and stellar physics!

departments.name: Geological Sciences
departments.description: The Franklin Mountains run right through UTEP's campus — a living geology textbook. UTEP geoscientists study earthquakes, groundwater, minerals, and ancient climates.
fun_facts: The Franklin Mountains on UTEP's campus are one of the few mountain ranges located entirely within a U.S. city.
UTEP geologists use the Franklin Mountains as a natural outdoor research lab — students can walk to their study site!
UTEP researchers study ancient fossils found in West Texas that are hundreds of millions of years old.

connections.mountain: Mountains tell the story of Earth's history — UTEP geologists study the Franklin Mountains right on campus!
connections.volcano: Volcanoes shaped the landscape of West Texas millions of years ago — UTEP geologists study this fiery past!
connections.dinosaur: Dinosaur fossils have been found across West Texas — UTEP geologists study the ancient life preserved in these rocks!
connections.rock: Every rock tells a story — UTEP geologists read the history of Earth in the stones of the Chihuahuan Desert!

departments.name: Mathematical Sciences
departments.description: UTEP mathematicians develop models that solve real-world problems — from predicting disease spread to optimizing traffic flow along the border.
fun_facts: UTEP math professors use statistics to model disease outbreaks and help public health officials on the border.
UTEP's math department runs tutoring and outreach programs that help thousands of El Paso students succeed.
Applied math at UTEP helps engineers, scientists, and business leaders make better decisions using data.

connections.triangle: Triangles are the building blocks of geometry — UTEP mathematicians use shapes to model the real world!
connections.circle: Circles appear everywhere in nature and engineering — UTEP math powers the science behind them!

departments.name: Environmental Science & Engineering
departments.description: UTEP environmental scientists study air quality, water treatment, and renewable energy. The El Paso–Juárez airshed is a shared environment that requires cross-border collaboration.
fun_facts: UTEP researchers monitor the air quality shared between El Paso and Juárez, protecting millions of people.
El Paso gets over 300 days of sunshine — UTEP researchers are making it a hub for solar energy development.
UTEP environmental engineers are developing affordable water purification systems for desert communities.

connections.rain: Rain is precious in the desert — UTEP environmental scientists study how to protect every drop of water!
connections.cloud: Clouds carry weather patterns across the border — UTEP scientists study climate to protect both El Paso and Juárez!
connections.rainbow: Rainbows happen when light meets water — UTEP scientists study both optics and water resources!

departments.name: Nursing
departments.description: UTEP's School of Nursing prepares nurses to serve the medically underserved U.S.-Mexico border community, one of the most health-challenged regions in the country.
fun_facts: UTEP nursing graduates serve communities where there is a critical shortage of healthcare providers.
UTEP's nursing program includes clinical rotations at hospitals throughout El Paso and the border region.
UTEP nursing students participate in free health clinics that serve uninsured border residents.

connections.heart: The heart pumps life through your body — UTEP nurses learn to care for hearts and save lives!
connections.stethoscope: Stethoscopes help doctors listen to your body — UTEP trains nurses who use them to keep people healthy!

departments.name: Kinesiology
departments.description: UTEP's kinesiology department studies how the human body moves, exercises, and recovers from injury. The famous UTEP Miners athletics program is a living lab.
fun_facts: UTEP's 1966 men's basketball team was the first team with an all-Black starting lineup to win the NCAA championship — changing sports history forever.
UTEP kinesiology students work directly with Miners athletes, applying science to improve athletic performance.
UTEP's biomechanics lab uses motion-capture cameras (like in movies!) to study how people walk, run, and jump.

connections.basketball: Basketball is science in motion — UTEP's 1966 team changed sports history by winning the NCAA championship!
connections.soccer ball: Soccer requires incredible athleticism — UTEP kinesiologists study how athletes move and perform!
connections.bicycle: Cycling is a full-body workout — UTEP exercise scientists study how your body produces energy and endurance!

departments.name: Public Health
departments.description: UTEP public health researchers tackle health challenges unique to the U.S.-Mexico border — diabetes, air pollution-related illness, and access to healthcare.
fun_facts: UTEP's border health research has received tens of millions in NIH funding to address health equity.
UTEP public health students conduct health screenings at community events throughout El Paso.
UTEP researchers study why diabetes rates are much higher on the border and how to fix it.

connections.pill: Medicine keeps people healthy — UTEP public health researchers work to make sure everyone on the border has access to care!
connections.apple: An apple a day! UTEP public health researchers study nutrition and disease prevention in border communities.

departments.name: Rehabilitation Sciences
departments.description: UTEP's rehab sciences help people recover from injuries and disabilities. Researchers develop affordable prosthetics and therapy techniques for the border region.
fun_facts: UTEP researchers have developed low-cost 3D-printed prosthetic hands for patients on both sides of the border.
UTEP's speech therapy program serves bilingual children in El Paso who need help communicating in two languages.
UTEP physical therapy students provide free rehabilitation services at community clinics in El Paso.

connections.hand: Hands let us create and connect — UTEP researchers 3D-print prosthetic hands for people who need them!
connections.wheelchair: Wheelchairs give people freedom — UTEP rehab scientists work to make mobility accessible for everyone!

colleges.name: Woody L. Hunt College of Business

departments.name: Finance & Economics
departments.description: UTEP's business college, named after El Paso entrepreneur Woody Hunt, prepares students for the U.S.-Mexico border economy — one of the world's most active trade corridors.
fun_facts: The El Paso–Juárez region processes over $100 billion in annual cross-border trade.
UTEP business students study international trade firsthand — the Paso del Norte bridge to Mexico is minutes from campus.
UTEP's economics research helps city leaders understand and grow the El Paso border economy.

connections.money: Money makes the economy move — UTEP business students study one of the world's busiest trade corridors right on the border!
connections.chart: Charts track how businesses grow — UTEP economists study the $100 billion El Paso–Juárez trade economy!

departments.name: Marketing & Management
departments.description: UTEP's management programs train entrepreneurs and business leaders who serve the bilingual, binational border market.
fun_facts: UTEP's Mike Loya Center for Innovation and Commerce helps students launch real startup businesses.
UTEP business students learn to operate in a bilingual market — a huge advantage in the global economy.
Many UTEP business graduates start companies that operate on both sides of the U.S.-Mexico border.

connections.lightbulb: Lightbulbs represent ideas — UTEP's innovation center helps students turn ideas into real businesses!
connections.shopping cart: Shopping is powered by supply chains — UTEP students learn how products travel across the border to reach stores!

departments.name: Teacher Education
departments.description: UTEP trains bilingual teachers who serve El Paso's diverse schools. The college focuses on equity-centered education for border communities.
fun_facts: UTEP produces more bilingual certified teachers than almost any other university in Texas.
Over 80% of El Paso students speak Spanish at home — UTEP trains teachers who can teach in both languages.
UTEP education students start working in real classrooms during their first year of training.

connections.book: Books open up worlds — UTEP trains bilingual teachers who help kids in El Paso read in two languages!
connections.pencil: Pencils are the start of every idea — UTEP trains thousands of teachers who inspire the next generation!
connections.school: Schools shape the future — UTEP prepares more bilingual teachers than almost any university in Texas!

departments.name: Educational Leadership & Foundations
departments.description: UTEP's education leadership program trains principals, administrators, and policy researchers who shape schools across the border region.
fun_facts: UTEP's education PhD program is nationally ranked for equity-centered research in schools serving Latino communities.
UTEP has helped El Paso's high school graduation rate rise significantly over the past decade.
Many El Paso school principals and superintendents are UTEP graduates.

connections.graduation cap: Graduation caps celebrate achievement — UTEP helps more border students reach that moment!
connections.trophy: Trophies celebrate winners — UTEP education leaders help every student succeed, not just the ones on top!

departments.name: Art
departments.description: UTEP's art department reflects the border region's rich cultural identity, blending Mexican, Indigenous, and American artistic traditions. The Rubin Center hosts world-class exhibitions.
fun_facts: UTEP's Stanlee and Gerald Rubin Center for the Visual Arts hosts world-class contemporary art exhibitions on campus.
UTEP art students create murals around El Paso that celebrate the city's unique border culture and history.
UTEP's digital art program teaches students to create art using the same tools used in Hollywood movies and video games.

connections.paint brush: Paintbrushes bring imagination to life — UTEP artists create works that celebrate El Paso's border culture!
connections.smiley face: Art makes people smile — UTEP artists create murals and installations all around El Paso!

departments.name: Music
departments.description: UTEP's music program celebrates El Paso's cross-cultural musical heritage — from mariachi and norteño to classical and jazz.
fun_facts: El Paso is a UNESCO Creative City of Music — UTEP musicians play a key role in this international recognition.
UTEP has a mariachi ensemble that performs at events across the Southwest, celebrating Mexican musical traditions.
UTEP's music education program trains teachers who bring music to schools throughout the border region.

connections.guitar: Guitars are the soul of border music — UTEP's music program celebrates El Paso's mariachi and norteño traditions!
connections.music note: Music notes carry melodies across cultures — El Paso is a UNESCO Creative City of Music thanks partly to UTEP!
connections.drum: Drums set the rhythm — UTEP musicians perform everything from mariachi to jazz to classical!

departments.name: Theatre & Dance
departments.description: UTEP's theatre and dance programs train performers and designers who tell stories reflecting the border region's rich cultural mix.
fun_facts: UTEP's Dinner Theatre is one of the longest-running dinner theatres in the Southwest.
UTEP dance students perform works that blend American modern dance with Mexican folk dance traditions.
UTEP theatre students design sets, costumes, and lighting for multiple full productions every year.

connections.mask: Masks transform performers — UTEP's theatre program tells stories that celebrate border culture!
connections.dancer: Dance is storytelling through movement — UTEP dancers blend American and Mexican dance traditions!

departments.name: Communication
departments.description: UTEP's communication department trains journalists, filmmakers, and media professionals who tell the stories of the border region to the world.
fun_facts: UTEP journalism students report real news for Borderzine, a bilingual news outlet covering the El Paso–Juárez border.
UTEP's film program has produced students whose work has screened at international film festivals.
UTEP communication graduates work at major news outlets, PR firms, and media companies across the country.

connections.tv: TV brings stories into your home — UTEP trains journalists who report on the U.S.-Mexico border!
connections.phone: Phones connect the world — UTEP digital media students learn to create content that reaches millions!

departments.name: History
departments.description: UTEP historians study the rich, complex history of the U.S.-Mexico border — from ancient Indigenous peoples to modern immigration and trade.
fun_facts: UTEP was founded in 1914 as the Texas State School of Mines — over 110 years of history on the border.
UTEP's campus architecture is designed in the Bhutanese style — inspired by a National Geographic photo of monasteries in Bhutan.
UTEP's Institute of Oral History has recorded thousands of hours of stories from border residents.

connections.castle: Castles are history you can see — UTEP's own campus looks like a Bhutanese monastery, unique in America!
connections.flag: Flags represent nations — UTEP sits where two nations meet, studying 400+ years of border history!
connections.map: Maps show where we are — UTEP historians map the fascinating history of the U.S.-Mexico border!

departments.name: Languages & Linguistics
departments.description: UTEP's linguistics program studies bilingualism and language along the border, where millions of people switch between English and Spanish daily.
fun_facts: El Paso is one of the most bilingual cities in America — over 80% of residents speak Spanish, making it a living linguistics lab.
UTEP linguists study code-switching — how border residents blend English and Spanish in everyday conversation.
UTEP's translation program trains professional translators who help hospitals, courts, and businesses communicate across languages.

connections.speech bubble: Speech bubbles hold words — UTEP linguists study how El Paso residents communicate in two languages every day!
connections.globe: The globe has thousands of languages — UTEP studies how bilingualism makes border communities stronger!
